foundations.subject: surgery
foundations.reason: Bismuth-Strasberg classification of strictures requires precise knowledge of biliary confluence anatomy

foundations.subject: surgery
foundations.reason: Cholecystectomy for gallstones is the most common cause of iatrogenic biliary stricture

foundations.subject: surgery
foundations.reason: Post-cholecystectomy bile duct injury is the primary etiology leading to benign biliary stricture formation

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Biliary strictures present with progressive obstructive jaundice requiring structured evaluation with MRCP/ERCP

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Strictures cause biliary stasis predisposing to recurrent cholangitis episodes requiring drainage

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Malignant strictures from gallbladder cancer must be differentiated from benign post-surgical strictures
